meta.title: Iran and Latin America: What Next after Chavez?
meta.description: For some years now, the Iranian government has sought to develop new ties with potential allies in Latin America—aided and abetted by Venezuelan strongman Hugo Chavez—particularly with Bolivia, Ecuador, and Nicaragua. Lately, with Chavez's health in question, Iran has sought another beachhead into Latin America by strengthening its ties to Argentina. Alongside its anti-U.S. strategic partnerships, Argentina's Kirchner administration has drawn sharp criticism for its financial recklessness, viola
